title: Large Language Models for Retrieval Augmented Generation
items.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study of retrieval augmented generation systems using large language models. We evaluate various retrieval strategies and their impact on model performance across multiple benchmarks.
subject: Computer Science
Machine Learning

title: Agentic Retrieval Systems with Neural Networks
items.abstract: We propose a novel agentic framework for building intelligent retrieval systems. Our approach demonstrates significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency compared to baseline methods.
subject: Natural Language Processing
Information Retrieval

title: Fine-tuning Transformers for Semantic Search
items.abstract: This work explores fine-tuning strategies for transformer models in semantic search applications. We demonstrate how transfer learning can improve performance with limited labeled data.
subject: Deep Learning
Neural Networks

title: Neural Scaling Laws in Language Models
items.abstract: We investigate neural scaling laws in language models for retrieval tasks. Our analysis reveals important insights about model capacity and performance relationships.
subject: Text Mining
Data Science

title: Reinforcement Learning for Information Retrieval
items.abstract: This paper introduces a reinforcement learning approach to optimize retrieval systems. Experimental results show substantial improvements over supervised baselines.
subject: Artificial Intelligence
Computational Linguistics

title: Knowledge Graphs and Vector Embeddings
items.abstract: We present methods for integrating knowledge graphs with vector embeddings for enhanced retrieval. The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks.
subject: Information Systems
Search

title: Prompt Engineering for LLM-based Systems
items.abstract: This work focuses on prompt engineering techniques for improving LLM-based retrieval systems. We provide practical guidelines for prompt design and optimization.
subject: Computer Science
Machine Learning

title: Efficient Retrieval with Dense Passage Vectors
items.abstract: We propose efficient retrieval methods using dense passage vectors. Our approach reduces memory requirements while maintaining competitive performance.
subject: Natural Language Processing
Information Retrieval

title: Multi-hop Reasoning in Retrieval Augmented Systems
items.abstract: This paper addresses multi-hop reasoning in retrieval augmented systems. We introduce new evaluation metrics and benchmarks for this challenging task.
subject: Deep Learning
Neural Networks

title: Hybrid Search: Dense and Sparse Retrieval
items.abstract: We explore hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval methods. Experimental results demonstrate the benefits of multi-strategy approaches.
